datasets.name: ims
physics_assumptions: The approximately 6000 lbf radial load and 2000 rpm are test-rig conditions; verify them against the IMS documentation used in the thesis.
Contact pressure, dynamic capacity, fatigue limit, and lubricant properties are explicit calibration assumptions because they are not measured in the raw vibration snapshots.
Strong-physics results must be reported with a sensitivity range over these assumed quantities.

datasets.name: pronostia
physics_assumptions: The three PRONOSTIA operating regimes have different loads; the checked-in global value is an initial effective-load approximation and should be replaced by run-specific metadata for final experiments.
Contact pressure, bearing capacity, and lubricant properties are calibration assumptions unless verified from the bearing specification.
Temperature physics is automatically masked because this dataset contains vibration rather than measured bearing temperature.

datasets.name: kaist_vibration_temperature
datasets.path: Datasets/Ball Bearing Vibration, and Temperature Run-to-Failure Dataset/Vibration_Bearing_RuntoFailure
physics_assumptions: This is one run-to-failure trajectory; its temporal split is a within-run forecasting benchmark, not evidence of cross-bearing generalization.
The recorded radial load is used as an initial equivalent-load approximation even though the rig also applies axial load.
Contact pressure, capacity, and lubricant properties require calibration from the bearing geometry/specification.

datasets.name: synthetic_varying_degradation
physics_assumptions: The supplied Data.mat is MATLAB v7.3/HDF5 and must first be converted to the documented standardized feature-table contract.
The source documentation describes 28 training and 12 test lives; preserve that official partition during conversion.

datasets.name: cwru_variants
physics_assumptions: CWRU fault samples are excluded from direct RUL regression because they are not run-to-failure trajectories with defensible RUL labels.

training.mixed_precision_rationale: Run 4 preserves Run 3 full-float training; physics derivatives remain in FP32 and numerical conditions are not changed.

crack_growth.source_note: Stage-2 rolling-contact-fatigue relation reported in the reviewed RCF crack-propagation paper; units and stress calibration must be verified for each bearing.

bearing_life.source_note: L10/Palmgren-Miner structure is physical. The differentiable aSKF expression is a calibratable surrogate for catalog lookup curves.

thermolubrication.source_note: Temperature-to-viscosity and contamination modifiers preserve the relationships in the reviewed bearing-fatigue study but require lubricant-specific calibration.

experiment.name: IMS fixed-model cross-bearing robustness
experiment.goal: Measure how the Run 3 model ranking changes when each of the four IMS trajectories is held out as test once.
experiment.evidence: Run 3 LSTM achieved mean test RMSE 0.144667, but the validation-selected Strong-PINN had a 9.7x validation/test gap and collapsed toward high RUL. Validation/test feature shifts indicate that one split is insufficient.
experiment.hypothesis: The Run 3 ranking and Strong-PINN failure are materially split-dependent; balanced held-out-bearing folds will reveal cross-bearing variance.
changed_from_previous: Replace the single IMS split with four predeclared held-out-bearing folds.
Enable only the frozen Run 3 Strong-PINN winner instead of recalibrating.
Record best-checkpoint and final-epoch test evidence for every fixed job.
held_constant: IMS feature cache and RUL labels
training-only StandardScaler fitting within each fold
AdamW learning rate 0.0005, batch size 64, and early stopping
experiment.primary_metric: Macro mean normalized RMSE: equal weight to each test bearing after averaging the three seeds within that bearing.
secondary_metrics: per-bearing MAE, MSE, RMSE, and R2
experiment.success_criterion: All 36 fixed jobs must complete with reproducible finite metrics. The Run 3 ranking is considered robust only if LSTM has the lowest macro RMSE and beats both PINNs in at least three of four held-out bearings. Strong-PINN portability additionally requires absolute late-life bias below 0.25 in at least three folds.
known_risks: Only four IMS trajectories are available, so each fold trains on two runs.
Run durations differ, making normalized RMSE the primary cross-fold metric.
The frozen physical parameters include documented unmeasured assumptions.
Cross-validation uses all four trajectories as test across folds and is not an external-dataset validation.

repository.note: The Colab notebook requires a 40-character commit SHA and checks out that exact revision under /content before importing thesis_work.

cross_bearing.validation_policy: Fixed balanced assignment: every trajectory is test once and validation once; the remaining two trajectories train. Validation is used only for scheduler and early stopping.
cross_bearing.test_policy: All model configurations and weights are frozen before any fold runs. Test-fold metrics are never used to change a model within EXP-004.
cross_bearing.primary_aggregation: Macro mean of the four per-bearing, three-seed mean normalized RMSE values.
